revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:石城国造]]
'''Iwaki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (石城国造, also read as Iwaki Kokuzō) was the {{ill|Kuni no miyatsuko|en|Kuni no miyatsuko|qid=Q2483673|lt=Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Kuni no miyatsuko}} who governed the region known as {{ill|Iwaki Province (718)|en|Iwaki Province (718)|qid=Q1192809|lt=Iwaki Province|1=Iwaki Province (718)}} (later part of southern {{ill|Mutsu Province|en|Mutsu Province|qid=Q907389|lt=Mutsu Province|1=Mutsu Province}} under the Ritsuryō system).
== Overview ==
=== Ancestry === * According to the ''{{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|qid=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}}'' (Chronicle of Former Kings), the lineage begins with '''{{ill|Tatekoromikoto|ja|建許呂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatekoromikoto|qq=}}''', who was appointed as Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|en|Emperor Seimu|qid=Q316672|lt=Emperor Seimu|1=Emperor Seimu}}. * In the fragment of the ''{{ill|Fudoki|en|Fudoki|qid=Q1194534|lt=Fudoki|1=Fudoki}}'' of Mutsu Province, the name of '''Iwaki-hiko''' (磐城彦) appears as a Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The ruling clan was the '''{{ill|Iwaki clan|en|Iwaki clan|qid=Q11474314|lt=Iwaki clan|1=Iwaki clan}}''' (石城氏, Iwaki-uji), bearing the hereditary title ({{ill|Kabane|en|Kabane|qid=Q1664099|lt=Kabane|1=Kabane}}) of '''Ason''' (直). * The ''Kuni no Miyatsuko Honki'' states that ''Tatekoromikoto'', a descendant of {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|天津彦根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アマツヒコネ}}, was the ancestor of the Iwaki no Kuni no Miyatsuko. * The ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}'' records that {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|qid=Q200188|lt=Kamuyamato Iwarebiko|1=Emperor Jimmu}}’s son, {{ill|Kamuyaimimi|en|Kamuyaimimi|qid=Q11588756|lt=Kamiyai-mimi no Mikoto|1=Kamuyaimimi}}, was the ancestor of the Ōomi, the Dōoku Iwaki no Kuni no Miyatsuko, among others.
== Headquarters ==
=== Territory === The Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as '''Iwaki Province''' (石城国), corresponding to the later '''{{ill|Iwaki District, Mutsu Province|ja|石城郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaki District, Mutsu Province|qq=}}''' in {{ill|Mutsu Province|en|Mutsu Province|qid=Q907389|lt=Mutsu Province|1=Mutsu Province}}. This roughly equates to the modern {{ill|Hamadōri|en|Hamadōri|qid=Q845466|lt=Hamadōri region|1=Hamadōri}} in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|qid=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}, particularly from the northern part of {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|en|Iwaki, Fukushima|qid=Q274384|lt=Iwaki, Fukushima|1=Iwaki, Fukushima}} to {{ill|Ōkuma, Fukushima|en|Ōkuma, Fukushima|qid=Q385311|lt=Ōkuma, Fukushima|1=Ōkuma, Fukushima}}.
By the mid-7th century, the region merged with the domains of {{ill|Michi no Oku Kikuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道奥菊多国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michi no Oku Kikuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (to the south) and {{ill|Taka no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taka no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} to form {{ill|Taga Province|ja|多珂国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taga Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 高国造}}. The northern boundary of the Iwaki domain was marked by ''Kuruma Village'' (苦麻の村) in what is now Ōkuma, as mentioned in the ''{{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi Fudoki|qq=}}''.
== Ancestral Deities ==
* '''{{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine|en|Ōkunitama Shrine|qid=Q611678|lt=Ōkunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|1=Ōkunitama Shrine}}''' Located in the historical {{ill|Kunitama district|ja|国魂郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama district|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Iwaki District, at 26 Miyamae, Sugawara, {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|en|Iwaki, Fukushima|qid=Q274384|lt=Iwaki, Fukushima|1=Iwaki, Fukushima}}, Fukushima Prefecture.
=== Related Shrines === * '''{{ill|Iwaki Kokuzō Shrine|ja|磐城国造神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaki Kokuzō Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' Situated in {{ill|Onahama, Iwaki|ja|小名浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onahama, Iwaki|qq=}}, enshrining {{ill|Tatekoromikoto|ja|建許呂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatekoromikoto|qq=}}.
=== Tomb === * '''{{ill|Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwaki)|ja|甲塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwaki)|qq=}}''' A round Kofun (円墳), 8 meters high with a diameter of 37 meters, believed to be the tomb of Tatekoromikoto, located 200 meters east of Ōkunitama Shrine. It is a designated National Historic Site.
== Descendants ==
* '''Ishikawa no Hyoujō Shikoseki''' According to the ''Hitachi Fudoki,'' in 653 (9th year of {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|en|Emperor Kōtoku|qid=Q317980|lt=Emperor Kōtoku|1=Emperor Kōtoku}}), Shikoseki petitioned the Eastern Governor with Ishikawa no Miyabe of Taga Province for administrative division.
* '''Ō Iwaki no Omi Yamagishi''' In 769 (3rd year of {{ill|Jingo-keiun|en|Jingo-keiun|qid=Q1373049|lt=Jingō-keiun|1=Jingo-keiun}}), he held the court Rank of Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade (外正六位上) and changed his family name from Takebe (丈部) to Ō Iwaki no Omi.
* '''Ō Iwaki no Omi Oishiki''' Granted the Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (外従五位下) in 782 (2nd year of {{ill|Ten'ō|ja|天応 (日本)|1=Ten'ō|2=ja|3=天応 (日本)|qq=}}{{citation needed|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten'ō|qq=}}).
* '''Iwaki no Omi Chisada''' Mentioned in lacquered documents from {{ill|Taga Castle|en|Taga Castle|qid=Q870867|lt=Tagajō|1=Taga Castle}} dated to 780 (11th year of {{ill|Hōki|en|Hōki|qid=Q18520676|lt=Hōki|1=Hōki}}).<ref name="Hirakawa">{{Cite journal|author=Minami Hirakawa|title=Short Essay on Satotoji: From the Second Wooden Tablet of the Aratame Site in Iwaki|journal=Bulletin of the National Museum of Japanese History|date=1996-02|doi=10.15024/00000757}}</ref>
* '''Iwaki no Omi Tōsei''' Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (外従五位下) in 826 (3rd year of {{ill|Tenchō|en|Tenchō|qid=Q1373138|lt=Tenchō|1=Tenchō}}).
* '''Iwaki no Omi Yūkō''', '''Iwaki no Omi Sadamichi''', '''Iwaki no Omi Tomonari''', '''Iwaki no Omi Akiō''' According to the ''{{ill|Shoku Nihon Kōki|en|Shoku Nihon Kōki|qid=Q1041301|lt=Shoku Nihon Kōki|1=Shoku Nihon Kōki}}'', in 840 (11th year of {{ill|Jōwa|en|Jōwa|qid=Q3577279|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa}}), Yūkō, the Governor of Iwaki District, was granted the surname ''Abe Iwaki no Omi.''
* '''{{ill|Iwaki clan|en|Iwaki clan|qid=Q11474314|lt=Iwaki clan|1=Iwaki clan}} / {{ill|Iwaki clan|1=:en:Iwaki clan|lt=Iwaki clan|ja|磐城氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaki clan|13=User:Immanuelle/:en:Iwaki clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' Also known as the ''Kunitama clan,'' serving as chief priests of Ōkunitama Shrine.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}} * {{ill|Iwaki Province (718)|en|Iwaki Province (718)|qid=Q1192809|lt=Iwaki Province|1=Iwaki Province (718)}} – Established in 718 and abolished in the 720s, later incorporated into {{ill|Mutsu Province|en|Mutsu Province|qid=Q907389|lt=Mutsu Province|1=Mutsu Province}}. * {{ill|Iwaki District, Mutsu Province|ja|石城郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaki District, Mutsu Province|qq=}} (formerly ''Iwaki no Koori'') * {{ill|Iwaki District, Fukushima|ja|磐城郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaki District, Fukushima|qq=}} * {{ill|Mutsu Province|en|Mutsu Province|qid=Q907389|lt=Mutsu Province|1=Mutsu Province}} * {{ill|Michi no Oku Kikuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道奥菊多国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michi no Oku Kikuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} / {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuta District|qq=}} * {{ill|Taka no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taka no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} / {{ill|Taga District, Ibaraki|en|Taga District, Ibaraki|qid=Q11431107|lt=Taga District|1=Taga District, Ibaraki}} * {{ill|Taga Province|ja|多珂国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taga Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 高国造}} * {{ill|Fukushima Daiichi Nuclear Power Plant|en|Fukushima Daiichi Nuclear Power Plant|qid=Q114295|lt=Fukushima Daiichi Nuclear Power Plant|1=Fukushima Daiichi Nuclear Power Plant}} – Located near the historical ''Kuruma Village,'' the northernmost part of Iwaki's territory. * {{ill|Iwaki Yumoto Onsen|en|Iwaki Yumoto Onsen|qid=Q11260179|lt=Iwaki Yumoto Onsen|1=Iwaki Yumoto Onsen}} – Located near the historical ''Yumoto Keisei,'' marking the southern limit of the region.
* [[Michinoku Kikuta Kuni-no-Miyatsuko]] ...r''' (菊多関): Kikuta (Kikuta District) → {{ill|Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Iwakiyama District) → {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Hyouge D * {{ill|Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} ... 5 KB (612 words) - 00:19, 18
January 2025 * [[Tsukamae Kofun]] * {{ill|Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|qq=}} ... 5 KB (646 words) - 05:31, 20
January 2025 Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko ...atsuko|ja|道奥菊多国造}} (Kikuta District) - {{ill|Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Ishikami District) - {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Sometabe ... 5 KB (724 words) - 07:08, 19
January 2025
{{translated page|ja|石城国造|version=104636643|comment=Imported full ja history}}

[[ja:%E7%9F%B3%E5%9F%8E%E5%9B%BD%E9%80%A0]]
